checks.finding: requirements.md Non-goals states test.yml staging is scoped to 'what Streams A (one new CI step) and D (the job-graph restructuring) require' and that 'Streams B and C (once unblocked) extend or add suites already covered by Stream A's or an existing registration path; they do not independently re-stage test.yml' (requirements.md Non-goals bullet, ~line 291-295) -- read literally this implies Stream B's new tests/guard-negative-corpus.tests.sh needs no CI-step registration of its own. But AC-019 (line 441-447) requires 'every new suite from Streams A and B' be registered, AC-020 (line 448-452) requires 'a CI step for every new suite from AC-019' (i.e. both A and B), Main Workflows item 2 for Stream B (line 557-558) itself says 'stage a CI step via human-copy (may share the SAME staged test.yml batch as Stream A's step, design.md decides)', and acceptance-tests.md TEST-020 (line 34) labels the shared batch only '(shared batch, Streams A + D)' with no mention of Stream B. investigation.md INV-006 established that test.yml has 'no wildcard or run-all delegation' and 'any new suite #123/#124 add must be explicitly wired into test.yml ... or it will silently never run in CI' -- exactly the registration-gap risk this feature's own top-listed Risk targets. A task author who takes the Non-goals sentence literally could conclude Stream B needs no independent test.yml wiring at all and ship its corpus never running in CI; a task author following AC-019/020/Main-Workflows would add a distinct step. The text does not resolve which reading is correct, and getting it wrong reproduces the exact registration-gap defect class the feature exists to close.

requirements.md Goals REQ-004 (line 229-237) requires Stream D to 'Restructure .github/workflows/test.yml's single test job into named deterministic-lane job(s) ... updating required-checks' needs: list to preserve BL-001's exact pass/fail semantics' and AC-017 (line 428-435) requires 'required-checks' needs: list is updated so every job that now covers a step formerly inside the single test job is present, directly or via a job-dependency chain' -- both presuppose the single 'test' job is split into multiple distinct GitHub Actions jobs whose names get added to needs:. acceptance-tests.md operationalizes the opposite: TEST-016 (line 30) states only that '.github/workflows/test.yml's test job steps each gain a [deterministic] name prefix' -- a cosmetic rename of step display-names inside the SAME single job, not a job split -- and TEST-017 (line 31) explicitly asserts 'required-checks: needs: [test, cli-hook-enforcement] membership confirmed unchanged.' If TEST-016/017 are followed literally, no new job exists and needs: is never updated, directly contradicting AC-017's 'needs: list is updated' requirement and leaving investigation.md INV-019's 'no lane boundary of any kind' problem unsolved. If AC-016/017 are instead followed literally (real job split + needs: update), TEST-017's own 'confirmed unchanged' assertion is violated. This is a direct conflict on BL-001, the one explicit preserved-behavior contract (required-checks branch-protection pass/fail semantics) this feature commits not to regress (Overview, Assumptions, investigation.md Baseline Behavior BL-001).

WFI-014 branch enumeration is applied thoroughly and correctly for both unblocked, testable requirements: REQ-001's 6-branch fallback chain (python3-present control, pwsh, powershell.exe, powershell, all-absent deny_unavailable, precedence-order) each get dedicated TEST-001..006, TEST-007 separately enumerates 2-emit-mode x 4-.ps1-reaching-branch = 8 named sub-cases (acceptance-tests.md line 21), and REQ-002's '3 classes x 4 runtimes x 3 tool_name shapes' language is honored literally as 12 independently-reported fixture+assertion pairs per class (TEST-008/009/010, lines 22-24, Notes lines 47-52: '36 total leaf assertions') plus an explicit cross-runtime parity aggregation (TEST-011). SKIP handling is explicit and named, not silent (Notes lines 68-70: a host lacking node causes only .js sub-cases to SKIP with a named reason, 'never a silent PASS'). One minor gap: requirements.md Edge Cases (lines 591-597) mandates that 'each fixture's setup step must assert the intended tool is genuinely absent (or genuinely a controlled stub) before asserting on the guard's decision, so a false-negative python3-absent premise ... does not silently pass by accident,' but no AC/TEST-ID independently verifies this precondition-check itself exists in the fixtures (AC-001..007/TEST-001..007 test only the resulting branch decision). This does not leave any required product behavior untested, so it does not block PASS, but a task reviewer should confirm this fixture-hygiene requirement is carried into task-level acceptance criteria.

All six Open Questions (requirements.md lines 679-723) are explicitly marked RESOLVED with a recorded decision and rationale (OQ-1: new unprotected suites not protected-suite edits; OQ-2: Stream C Blocked pending ADR-0010 Accepted; OQ-3: clean new namespace with cross-reference; OQ-4: moot, re-verified; OQ-5: preventive/structural option (a); OQ-6: inbound direction, sdd-bootstrap-scoped) -- none is left as a silent, unflagged assumption. The five-item Assumptions section (lines 637-675) each carries an explicit 'RE-VERIFY ... at implementation time' obligation (WFI-013 discipline) rather than being silently trusted, and the document's own 'Re-verification note' (lines 26-100) demonstrates this discipline was already exercised once during spec authoring (6 material drifts found and corrected against current HEAD rather than inherited stale from investigation.md, e.g. identity-ledger tail sequence 337 vs stale 319, quality-gate-cycle-limit registration in test.yml already applied). No hidden or unaddressed assumption was found that would block design or task decomposition.

Two governance/irreversible-change boundaries are in scope and testable. (1) Human-copy staging for the R-10-protected .github/workflows/test.yml (Field Definitions lines 505-510; Roles and Permissions: 'The agent NEVER writes .github/workflows/test.yml directly for any stream ... Human maintainer: ... validates and applies the staged ... human-copy candidate(s) as pre-merge commits') is made observable via AC-016 ('the LIVE file is confirmed unmodified by the agent at staging time') and AC-020 ('the live file's grep-based self-check for each new suite's basename is red until a human applies the staged candidate -- the designed fail-closed state, no staged-candidate fallback') -- both give a concrete, checkable signal that the agent has not bypassed the boundary. (2) ADR-0010's Status: Accepted is 'the sole unblock condition for Stream C's implementation ... this decision is NOT made by this spec or by any agent session' (Roles and Permissions), and OQ-2's resolution makes the boundary observable too: 'no tests/workflow-scenarios/ file is created, and no CHANGELOG.md entry for #125 is written, until then' -- Stream C's artifact absence is itself a checkable proxy for the approval gate holding.

The same REQ-004 conflict identified under CONTRADICTION (requirements.md AC-016/AC-017 vs acceptance-tests.md TEST-016/TEST-017) leaves the design reviewer unable to determine Stream D's actual deliverable without inventing it: a real multi-job CI restructuring (as Overview/Problems/Goals describe: 'named deterministic-lane job(s)', 'separates ... into named lanes', a fix for investigation.md INV-019's 'no lane boundary of any kind') or a cosmetic step-name-prefix rename inside the existing single 'test' job with an explicitly unchanged needs: list (as TEST-016/TEST-017 literally specify). These two readings carry materially different risk profiles for BL-001 (required-checks' branch-protection pass/fail semantics, the one preserved-behavior contract this feature commits not to regress) and different task-decomposition scope (job-graph edit + needs: update vs. a text-only step rename). Design.md would have to silently pick one interpretation rather than receiving an unambiguous product-behavior specification -- exactly the invented-behavior risk DOWNSTREAM-READINESS is meant to catch before implementation-policy review.
